videos.title: Couple lost suburban home, then built a better life on Ozarks homestead
videos.author: Kirsten Dirksen
videos.description:
After losing their suburban home — buried under three mortgages and then a flood — Catherine and Phillip Lightfoot decided not to start over the same way. They wanted the homestead they'd dreamed of since childhood, and they wanted to build it without owing anyone anything.

videos.title: Couldn’t afford a house. Now he builds his own underground Missouri home
videos.author: Kirsten Dirksen
videos.description:
Kyle Yoder couldn't afford to buy a home, so he's been building one himself — by hand, with earth, rubble, and reclaimed materials — on a shared piece of land in northern Missouri.

videos.title: Was told not to build underground. Ravaging tornado proved him right
videos.author: Kirsten Dirksen
videos.description:
When Tim and Monica open the front door of their underground home buried beneath a Missouri hillside, they step into a world most people can't fathom. Only one corner of the structure is visible. Everything else has disappeared into the earth.

videos.title: Living 35 ft underground: inside a preserved Titan II nuclear missile silo
videos.author: Kirsten Dirksen
videos.description:
For decades, four missileers at a time lived 35 feet underground in this Titan II command complex outside Tucson, Arizona - working shifts that took them even deeper, 145 feet down into the earth. It tested the limits of both body and mind.

videos.title: Family wanted to start over. They're building opt-in village for anyone to apply
videos.author: Kirsten Dirksen
videos.description:
Sarah and her family of 5 ditched the city grid for a radically self-sufficient life 45 minutes down a dirt road in northern Arizona. They're the first residents of Coslor Cove—an off-grid cooperative where 200 families are building true independence in community.
